meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve Basic App Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Break through beginner coding plateaus. Learn to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade portfolio projects today.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are coding manually instead of designing with AI. By integrating Antigravity for rapid UI prototyping and Claude Code for intelligent architectural scaffolding, you shift from writing syntax to engineering features. This workflow allows you to build high-impact, production-ready applications that define a world-class developer portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows, but your progress has stalled. You are trapped in the 'tutorial hell' loop, building yet another To-Do list that lacks depth, polish, and professional utility. The difference between a student and a senior engineer isn't just syntax; it is the ability to leverage intelligent systems to bridge the gap between abstract ideas and deployed architecture.

It is time to stop typing every character manually. By mastering AI-assisted design, you turn your editor into an extension of your intent. We will use Antigravity to visualize and construct complex components, while Claude Code acts as your pair programmer to handle the heavy lifting of backend integration. Your journey toward building real-world SaaS starts here.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, establish your data models and user flow. Use AI to validate your schema against industry standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea: a real-time collaborative workspace. Generate a clean database schema using Prisma and define the core API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project structure for a Next.js app with a Supabase backend based on these requirements.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Prototyping
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity UI components that match your design system, ensuring accessibility and responsiveness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI primitives. Include a sidebar, header, and main content area.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the layout components generated in Antigravity into the src/components folder.'

steps.title: Automate Component Logic
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate state management. Let Claude Code generate the hooks and event handlers for your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom React hook for managing global user authentication state with context and local storage persistence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a useAuth hook file and integrate it into the main App component.'

steps.title: Integrate API Services
steps.description: Connect your frontend to your backend services. Use AI to write robust error handling and loading states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a fetch wrapper for my Supabase API that includes request interceptors for token handling and generic error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Connect the login form to the auth service and add a toast notification on error.'

steps.title: Final QA and Refinement
steps.description: Use AI to conduct a code review of your entire repository, looking for performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security audit on my current project code. Suggest three optimizations for rendering performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review all files in the src directory for common security vulnerabilities and suggest fixes.'

pro_tips: Treat your AI as a junior developer: always provide context about your stack before asking for code.
Use 'iterative prompting'—never ask for a whole app at once, build it layer by layer (UI, then logic, then API).
Keep your Claude Code context clean by frequently committing small, logical chunks of code to your Git history.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'explain like I'm a senior dev' to get architectural advice rather than just snippet generation.
Always verify AI-generated code with unit tests; use Claude Code to generate the test files automatically.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to Vercel or Railway, then document your design process on a platform like Dev.to or LinkedIn to attract recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build beyond the basics. Stop waiting for the perfect time and start writing the code that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
